
**How has chemical reporting changed over time across different product categories?**

```{r}
#| label: data-wrangling

# Filter to valid years and aggregate by year and category
time_trends <- cscp_clean %>%
 filter(
   !is.na(report_year),
   report_year >= 2009,
   report_year <= 2020
 ) %>%
 group_by(report_year, primary_category) %>%
 summarise(
   report_count = n(),
   .groups = "drop"
 )

# Get top 4 categories
top_4_categories <- cscp_clean %>%
 group_by(primary_category) %>%
 summarise(total = n()) %>%
 arrange(desc(total)) %>%
 slice_head(n = 4) %>%
 pull(primary_category)

# Filter to top 4 categories and shorten names
time_trends_top <- time_trends %>%
 filter(primary_category %in% top_4_categories) %>%
 mutate(
   category_short = case_when(
     str_detect(primary_category, "Makeup") ~ "Makeup",
     str_detect(primary_category, "Skin Care") ~ "Skin Care",
     str_detect(primary_category, "Hair Care") ~ "Hair Care",
     str_detect(primary_category, "Sun") ~ "Sun Products",
     TRUE ~ primary_category
   )
 )
```

### Visualization: Line Chart

```{r}
#| label: rq4-line-chart
#| fig-width: 11
#| fig-height: 7
#| fig-alt: "Line chart showing chemical reporting trends by product category from 2009 to 2020"

#..........................create title..........................
title_rq4 <- glue::glue(
 "Chemical Reporting <span style='color:#e84a8a;'>Trends</span>
 by Product Category (2009-2020)"
)

#.........................create caption.........................
caption_rq4 <- glue::glue(
 "Source: California Safe Cosmetics Program (CSCP) | 2009-2020<br>
 <span style='font-family:fa-brands;'>{github_icon};</span> {github_username}"
)

#...................define colors for categories.................
category_colors <- c(
 "Makeup" = "#e84a8a",
 "Skin Care" = "#14b8a6",
 "Hair Care" = "#a855f7",
 "Sun Products" = "#fbbf24"
)

#..............................plot..............................
plot4_timeline <- time_trends_top %>%
 ggplot(aes(
   x = report_year,
   y = report_count,
   color = category_short
 )) +
 geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
 geom_point(size = 2.5) +
 scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(2009, 2020, 1)) +
 scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
 scale_color_manual(values = category_colors) +
 labs(
   title = title_rq4,
   subtitle = "Makeup products consistently have the highest number of chemical reports",
   x = "Year",
   y = "Number of Reports",
   color = NULL,
   caption = caption_rq4
 ) +
 theme_cosmetics_dark() +
 theme(
   legend.position = "bottom",
   axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
 ) +
 guides(color = guide_legend(nrow = 1))

plot4_timeline
```
